title: Source lineage for The Romance of Guy of Warwick: The First or Fourteenth-Century Version
summary: The checked-in aha2638 XML copies University of Michigan CME item AHA2638.0001.001, a 2006 uncorrected OCR encoding of a carrier assembled under a title-quarter sheet dated 25 November 1905 from Julius Zupitza's separately issued EETS parts of 1883, 1887, and 1891. The parallel edition combines several distinct textual streams: the Auchinleck first-couplet Guy, the Caius 107/176 composite couplet witness, the independent Auchinleck stanzaic Guy after marriage, and the Auchinleck Reinbrun continuation. Zupitza inherited Turnbull's 1840 text for the Auchinleck streams but used J. A. H. Murray's manuscript collation and his own corrections; Caius was transcribed directly. The defective Auchinleck opening is supplied from Anglo-Norman Gui de Warewic in Corpus Christi College Cambridge MS 50 and collated against Harley MS 3775 f. 15; the defective Reinbrun ending is supplied from Parker MS 50 f. 181r. Three Caius lines at printed p. 531 are supplied from CUL Ff.2.38 lines 9234-36. Complete NLS and Parker manuscript surrogates and complete scans/OCR of all three part issues, Turnbull's prior edition, Zupitza's 1875-76 Ff.2.38 edition, DIMEV data, and supporting Caius catalog/access evidence are cached with exact target-presence declarations. Physical, textual, editorial, scan, OCR, access, and rights layers remain separate.
review_notes: The edition title's singular 'version' is historical naming; the manifest preserves four medieval work/redaction identities and passage-scoped supplies.
The couplet-to-stanzaic boundary is shared on Auchinleck f. 146vb; the stanzaic-to-Reinbrun boundary is shared on f. 167rb.
Sloane MS 1044 is not given a direct target relation: it appears in Zupitza's separate 1875-76 apparatus and DIMEV's first-version census, but not in the inspected aha2638 XML or 1883-91 part OCR.
Negative online-facsimile and access findings are date-bounded to 2026-07-16.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for aha2638
entities.description: The exact XML artifact consumed by this repository's publication workflow and supplied through the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: The 1,046,959-byte XML contains 5 DIV1, 272 DIV2, 4 HEAD, 22,672 L, 670 LG, 1,033 MILESTONE, 6 P, 694 PB, and 663 NOTE1 elements.
The source header declares 694 linked 600-dpi TIFF G4 page images, but those U-M page images are not repository files.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item AHA2638.0001.001
entities.description: A 2006 TEI-in-Libraries Level 1 electronic text made by uncorrected OCR, with no content editing and page-image links, from the 1905 composite carrier of Zupitza's three parts.
notes: The XML language header says eng; direct content inspection distinguishes Middle English, inserted Anglo-Norman, and modern English editorial matter.

entities.label: University of Michigan AHA2638 page-image set
entities.description: The 694 600-dpi TIFF G4 page images historically linked by the U-M encoding. Exact current anonymous delivery could not be verified because the item routes returned a Cloudflare challenge on the review date.
entities.reproduction_format: 694 linked 600-dpi TIFF G4 page images reported in the encoding header

entities.label: Zupitza Guy of Warwick first-version composite with 1905 title-quarter sheet
entities.description: The later binding presentation immediately underlying the U-M encoding: a title-quarter sheet issued because members wished to bind the three previously published parts together. It is a carrier-level compilation, not a fourth publication of a single newly edited text.

date_statements.label: title-quarter-sheet issue
date_statements.value: Issued in 1905; Furnivall's explanatory statement dated 25 November 1905

bibliographic.citation: Julius Zupitza, ed., The Romance of Guy of Warwick: The First or 14th-Century Version, EETS Extra Series 42, 49, 59 (London, 1883, 1887, 1891), composite title-quarter sheet issued in 1905; Furnivall's explanatory statement dated 25 November 1905.
bibliographic.title: The Romance of Guy of Warwick: The First or 14th-Century Version
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Extra Series 42, 49, 59
bibliographic.publisher: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co., Limited

entities.label: Zupitza, Guy of Warwick first version, Part I (1883; notice dated 1885)
entities.description: The first separately issued parallel-text part, printed pp. 1-224. Its temporary notice is dated Berlin, 21 February 1885 despite the title's 1883 series date.

date_statements.label: publication
date_statements.value: 1883 title date; temporary notice dated 21 February 1885

bibliographic.citation: Julius Zupitza, ed., The Romance of Guy of Warwick: The First or 14th-Century Version, Part I, EETS Extra Series 42 (London, 1883; temporary notice 1885).

entities.label: Zupitza, Guy of Warwick first version, Part II (1887)
entities.description: The second separately issued parallel-text part, continuing printed pp. 225-383.

bibliographic.citation: Julius Zupitza, ed., The Romance of Guy of Warwick: The First or 14th-Century Version, Part II, EETS Extra Series 49 (London, 1887).

entities.label: Zupitza, Guy of Warwick first version, Part III (1891)
entities.description: The third separately issued part, printed pp. 384-674, completing the parallel Guy streams and adding Reinbrun with an Anglo-Norman ending supply.

bibliographic.citation: Julius Zupitza, ed., The Romance of Guy of Warwick: The First or 14th-Century Version, Part III, EETS Extra Series 59 (London, 1891).

entities.label: Turnbull, The Romances of Sir Guy of Warwick, and Rembrun His Son (1840)
entities.description: The Abbotsford Club edition of the Auchinleck couplet Guy, stanzaic Guy, and Reinbrun texts. It is Zupitza's immediate printed intermediary for those streams, subsequently corrected and collated against the manuscript.

bibliographic.citation: William B. D. D. Turnbull, ed., The Romances of Sir Guy of Warwick, and Rembrun His Son (Edinburgh: Abbotsford Club, 1840).
bibliographic.title: The Romances of Sir Guy of Warwick, and Rembrun His Son

entities.label: Zupitza, Guy of Warwick second or fifteenth-century version (1875-76)
entities.description: Zupitza's separate complete edition of the later couplet version from CUL Ff.2.38. It is supporting evidence for the exact source lines 9234-36 inserted into the aha2638 Caius stream, not the global source edition of aha2638.

bibliographic.citation: Julius Zupitza, ed., The Romance of Guy of Warwick: The Second or 15th-Century Version, EETS Extra Series 25-26 (London, 1875-76).
bibliographic.title: The Romance of Guy of Warwick: The Second or 15th-Century Version

entities.label: Guy of Warwick, first couplet version (DIMEV 4907 / IMEV 3145)
entities.description: The conceptual first Middle English couplet redaction, conventionally 11,095 lines across its witnesses.

entities.label: Guy of Warwick, later couplet version (DIMEV 4908 / IMEV 3146)
entities.description: The conceptual 11,976-line later Middle English couplet redaction, complete in CUL Ff.2.38 and partially spliced into Caius 107/176.

entities.label: Stanzaic Guy of Warwick after marriage (DIMEV 1557 / IMEV 946)
entities.description: The independent 299-stanza Auchinleck account of Guy after marriage.

entities.label: Reinbrun, son of Guy of Warwick (DIMEV 2907 / IMEV 1754)
entities.description: The independent 127-stanza Middle English Reinbrun romance, surviving imperfectly in the Auchinleck manuscript.

entities.label: Gui de Warewic, Anglo-Norman romance tradition
entities.description: The wider Anglo-Norman romance tradition represented by Parker MS 50 and Harley MS 3775. Exact Parker passages repair the defective opening and ending in Zupitza's edition; no one surviving codex is asserted as the universal source of all Middle English redactions.

entities.label: National Library of Scotland, Advocates MS 19.2.1 (Auchinleck Manuscript)
entities.description: The principal physical witness for three distinct target works: first-couplet Guy at ff. 108ra-146vb, stanzaic Guy at ff. 146vb-167rb, and imperfect Reinbrun at ff. 167rb-175vb.

notes: Probably produced in London. This copying context is not independently transferred to work-level composition.

entities.label: NLS complete IIIF facsimile of Advocates MS 19.2.1
entities.description: A complete 734-canvas current NLS digital object. Target text occupies canvases 251-386, with shared boundaries visible on canvases 328 and 369.
entities.reproduction_format: 734 color IIIF canvases; complete local 1600-pixel bundle

entities.label: Cambridge, Corpus Christi College, Parker MS 50
entities.description: A late-thirteenth-century parchment codex containing Anglo-Norman Gui de Warewic. Zupitza directly copies its ff. 103ra-va for the defective Auchinleck opening and f. 181ra-rb for the defective Reinbrun ending.

physical_description.extent: 181 folios plus 8 preliminary or additional leaves

entities.label: Parker Library complete IIIF facsimile of MS 50
entities.description: The complete 386-canvas Parker digital surrogate; exact target supply passages map to canvases 217-218 (ff. 103r-v) and 373 (f. 181r).
entities.reproduction_format: 386 color IIIF canvases; complete local 1600-pixel bundle

entities.label: London, British Library, Harley MS 3775
entities.description: A 178-folio vellum codex containing Anglo-Norman Gui de Warewic at ff. 15r-26r. Zupitza collated only the opening Parker supply against f. 15 for the target edition.

identifiers.scheme: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue

entities.label: Gonville and Caius College MS 107/176
entities.description: The fifteenth-century composite couplet Guy witness transcribed in Zupitza's parallel stream at manuscript pp. 1-271. Modern analysis identifies it as a splice of first- and later-couplet redactions.

notes: James records donation by William Moore.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Ff.2.38
entities.description: A mid-fifteenth-century paper codex containing the complete 11,976-line later-couplet Guy at ff. 161r-231r. Only lines 9234-36 are directly inserted into aha2638, replacing three lines absent from Caius.

entities.label: DIMEV 2.0-alpha.1 records for the aha2638 textual streams
entities.description: Versioned repertory records 4907, 1557, 2907, and 4908, including exact work identities, incipits, explicits, structures, witness locators, bibliography, and Caius splice notes.

entities.label: Database of Middle English Romance: Guy of Warwick
entities.description: Current scholarly synthesis distinguishing five independent redactions, the three narratively continuous Auchinleck works, and the Caius first/later-couplet splice.

entities.label: M. R. James, Gonville and Caius manuscript catalogue, volume 1
entities.description: Historical catalog containing the exact physical and item-level description for MS 107/176 on printed pp. 107-108.

bibliographic.citation: M. R. James, A Descriptive Catalogue of the Manuscripts in the Library of Gonville and Caius College, vol. 1 (Cambridge, 1907), 107-108.

access.access_method: Use the institutional IIIF record or Parker catalog in a browser; command-line retrieval of the catalog route was rejected by the web application firewall.
Use the exact Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue record.
The catalog says digitized-content access remains unavailable during recovery; monitor the exact record or ask about mediated images.
Consult in the British Library Manuscripts Reading Room; the exact catalog record requires a letter of introduction for access.
Ask the College Library for an existing complete color surrogate or a whole-object quotation, specifying all 136 folios and identifying manuscript pp. 1-271 separately.

local_copies.label: Gonville and Caius College copyright policy
notes: Access and reuse policy only; contains no target verse.
Historic collections are available by appointment to genuine researchers; no item-level public complete facsimile was verified.

access.access_method: Arrange an appointment with the College Library and justify scholarly need.
Download the complete catalog volume; inspect printed pp. 107-108 / PDF pp. 147-148.

local_copies.label: Complete James Caius catalog volume 1 PDF
notes: The volume describes MS 107/176 but does not reproduce the target verse text.

access.access_method: Use the D-MER manuscript record for current descriptive access.
Request the entire current physical codex from CUL Imaging Services; identify Guy ff. 161r-231r and source lines 9234-36 separately.
notes: No current public complete manuscript facsimile was verified. The 1979 printed monochrome facsimile was identified bibliographically but not found as a verified public download.

access.access_method: Download the exact versioned release archive from Zenodo and inspect the four target records.

rights.component: Pinned Kyvero-Press/CME XML, OCR, and markup layer
rights.basis: The pinned repository LICENSE expressly dedicates software, including source and binary code, but does not clearly state that historical XML, OCR, editorial markup, or data are covered. Public-domain status of the medieval texts and old printed editions does not by itself settle rights in this digital encoding layer.

rights.component: Underlying 1840, 1875-91, and 1905 printed texts and medieval source texts
rights.basis: All identified print components were published no later than 1905; the medieval texts are centuries older. This does not license modern scans, OCR, catalog prose, or manuscript photography.

rights.component: Underlying 1883 Part I edition text and editorial apparatus
rights.basis: This edition part was published in 1883. Its United States public-domain status does not extend to modern scans, OCR, or provider presentation layers.

rights.basis: This edition part was published in 1887. Its United States public-domain status does not extend to modern scans, OCR, or provider presentation layers.

rights.basis: Turnbull's edition was published in 1840. Its United States public-domain status does not extend to modern scans, OCR, or provider presentation layers.

rights.basis: This edition was published in 1875-76. Its United States public-domain status does not extend to modern scans, OCR, or provider presentation layers.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Turnbull's prior Auchinleck text, Murray's manuscript collation, the Caius manuscript, and additional passage witnesses
editorial_practices.representation: Parallel edited texts with manuscript folio/page milestones, supplied readings, and notes
editorial_practices.description: Zupitza retained Turnbull as the immediate base for the Auchinleck streams but corrected it from J. A. H. Murray's manuscript collation and his own work; he transcribed Caius directly. Passage-scoped supplies and collations are represented by separate relations.
editorial_practices.scope: All three part issues, with source roles varying by textual stream

editorial_practices.source_feature: Lacunae in the Auchinleck opening, Caius at three lines, and the Auchinleck Reinbrun ending
editorial_practices.representation: Bracketed Anglo-Norman and Middle English passages with source notes
editorial_practices.description: The opening is supplied from Parker MS 50 and collated against Harley 3775; three Caius lines are supplied from CUL Ff.2.38; the Reinbrun ending is supplied from Parker MS 50. These repairs are not silently attributed to the defective witnesses.
editorial_practices.scope: Opening pp. 2-8, Caius p. 531 lines 9515-17, and Reinbrun ending p. 674

editorial_practices.representation: Uncorrected OCR at TEI-in-Libraries Level 1 with linked page images
editorial_practices.description: U-M states that no OCR corrections or content editing were performed. The half title and back matter are omitted, while the composite title-quarter sheet and printed text pages 1-674 are retained.

editorial_practices.representation: One bindable carrier under a new title-quarter sheet
editorial_practices.description: Furnivall's signed note says the sheet was issued because members wished to bind together the three parts and no pupil had completed the broader multi-version edition.
editorial_practices.scope: Carrier title and assembly only; medieval textual boundaries unchanged

evidence.citation: Pinned Kyvero-Press/CME source XML aha2638.xml at commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.summary: Primary repository evidence for source identity, 2006 U-M production, uncorrected OCR practice, 694 image references, 1905 title-quarter sheet, all encoded medieval streams, exact source notices, page and manuscript milestones, apparatus, and structural counts.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME LICENSE at pinned commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.summary: Contains an Unlicense dedication expressly worded for software and source/binary code. Its applicability to historical XML, OCR, editorial markup, and data is not explicit, so it does not establish a public-domain grant for the exact aha2638 digital artifact.

evidence.citation: Zupitza composite title-quarter sheet and Furnivall note as encoded in AHA2638.0001.001.
evidence.summary: Direct evidence that the 1905 sheet was issued to let members bind the three separately published parts together; supplies composite title, editor, publisher, part dates, series numbers, and carrier extent.

evidence.citation: Julius Zupitza, ed., The Romance of Guy of Warwick: The First or 14th-Century Version, Part I, EETS ES 42 (1883; notice dated 1885).
evidence.locator: complete 240-page scan; printed pp. 1-224 and preliminary notice
evidence.summary: Primary part evidence for parallel layout, Murray's withdrawn co-editorship and collation, Turnbull base, direct Caius text, Parker opening supply, Harley f.15 collation, and publication chronology.

evidence.citation: Julius Zupitza, ed., The Romance of Guy of Warwick: The First or 14th-Century Version, Part II, EETS ES 49 (1887).
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for continuation of the parallel Auchinleck and Caius streams and the second part's exact printed bounds.

evidence.citation: Julius Zupitza, ed., The Romance of Guy of Warwick: The First or 14th-Century Version, Part III, EETS ES 59 (1891).
evidence.locator: complete 228-page scan; printed pp. 384-674; p. 531 supply; p. 631 Reinbrun start
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for the shared p.384 couplet/stanzaic boundary, completed Caius stream, exact three-line Ff.2.38 supply, Reinbrun text, and Parker ending supply.

evidence.citation: William B. D. D. Turnbull, ed., The Romances of Sir Guy of Warwick, and Rembrun His Son (Edinburgh, 1840).
evidence.summary: Complete prior edition of the Auchinleck Guy and Reinbrun streams and the immediate printed intermediary named by Zupitza.

evidence.citation: Julius Zupitza, ed., The Romance of Guy of Warwick: The Second or 15th-Century Version, EETS ES 25-26 (1875-76).
evidence.locator: complete 496-page scan; Ff.2.38 lines 9234-36 and notes to line 9237
evidence.summary: Complete direct edition of CUL Ff.2.38 used to verify the exact three-line source passage inserted into the aha2638 Caius stream; also shows Sloane 1044 only as collateral apparatus, not as a demonstrated aha2638 source.

evidence.citation: Linne R. Mooney, Daniel W. Mosser, Ian Cornelius, and Michael Johnston, The Digital Index of Middle English Verse: Data Files and Documentation, version 2.0-alpha.1 (2026), records 4907, 1557, 2907, and 4908.
evidence.locator: data/Records.xml records 4907, 1557, 2907, 4908; corresponding manuscript records; LICENSE
evidence.summary: Exact versioned evidence for four work identities, IMEV numbers, conventional extents, forms, incipits, explicits, witness ranges, the Caius splice, and CC BY 4.0 release terms.

evidence.citation: Database of Middle English Romance, Guy of Warwick.
evidence.summary: Current synthesis distinguishing five independent Middle English redactions, the three narratively continuous Auchinleck works, the Caius splice, CUL Ff.2.38's complete later-couplet text, circa-1300 date, questioned London localization, forms, witnesses, and subject keywords.

evidence.citation: Database of Middle English Romance, Cambridge University Library MS Ff.2.38.
evidence.summary: Current evidence for shelfmark, mid-fifteenth-century paper codex, dimensions, complete later-couplet Guy range and extent, and witness identity.

evidence.citation: National Library of Scotland, Advocates MS 19.2.1 and Auchinleck Manuscript contents pages.
evidence.summary: Official evidence for shelfmark, 1331-40 production, probable London origin, and the three target texts. The dedicated transcription and images correct the contents-table typo by showing stanzaic Guy beginning on f.146vb, not f.145vb.

evidence.citation: National Library of Scotland, complete IIIF manifest for Advocates MS 19.2.1.
evidence.summary: Exact official digital-object metadata for complete image count, shelfmark, date, Public Domain Mark, image services, and target boundary canvases.

evidence.citation: Direct inspection of the complete cached NLS Auchinleck IIIF bundle.
evidence.summary: Visually confirms the first surviving English opening at f.108r, the couplet/stanzaic boundary on f.146v, the stanzaic/Reinbrun boundary on f.167r, and Reinbrun's imperfect ending on f.175v.

evidence.citation: Parker Library, complete IIIF manifest for Corpus Christi College Cambridge MS 50.
evidence.summary: Exact institutional facsimile and metadata for the complete codex, target repair passages, attribution, and CC BY-NC 4.0 license.

evidence.citation: Direct inspection of Parker MS 50 target canvases in the complete cached IIIF bundle.
evidence.summary: Visually confirms the Anglo-Norman opening text on ff.103r-v and ending passage on f.181r used by Zupitza.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Harley MS 3775.
evidence.locator: Gui de Warewic ff.15r-26r; complete codex 178 folios
evidence.summary: Current official evidence for exact shelfmark, Anglo-Norman Guy locus, vellum support and extent, unavailable digital content during recovery, and onsite consultation requirement.

evidence.citation: M. R. James, A Descriptive Catalogue of the Manuscripts in the Library of Gonville and Caius College, vol. 1 (Cambridge, 1907).
evidence.locator: printed pp.107-108 / PDF pp.147-148; MS 107/176, manuscript pp.1-271
evidence.summary: Complete catalog volume confirming shelfmark, old designation, vellum support, 136-folio extent, two scribes, 30 lines per page, Guy incipit and explicit, and donation by William Moore.

evidence.citation: Gonville and Caius College Library information and Copyright Policy.
evidence.summary: Provides the current historic-collections appointment and enquiry route; the policy says supplied material is normally for private research and requires written application, purpose, format, and item-specific publication terms or fees.

evidence.citation: Cambridge University Library Imaging Services order and terms pages.
evidence.summary: Current route for fee-based whole-object image requests and evidence that private-study supply remains separate from reproduction and publication licensing.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan canonical item routes for AHA2638.0001.001.
evidence.summary: Canonical identifiers for the U-M item. Both routes returned a Cloudflare challenge to anonymous command-line access on 2026-07-16, so current page-image delivery and item-level rights were not inferred.

open_questions.question: Why does DIMEV record 4907 associate Zupitza 1883-91 with the Sloane MS 1044 fragment when neither the inspected aha2638 XML nor the three target part OCR files names Sloane 1044?
next_steps: Ask DIMEV whether the citation means global edition coverage, a lost apparatus reference, or bibliographic association; do not create a direct Sloane-to-aha2638 edge without page-level evidence.

open_questions.question: Can Gonville and Caius College provide a complete color surrogate of MS 107/176, including all 136 folios, bindings, flyleaves, and blanks, and what reuse terms would apply?
next_steps: Ask library@cai.cam.ac.uk for existing photography or a whole-object quote and written research/publication terms; identify pp.1-271 separately after delivery.

open_questions.question: Can Cambridge University Library provide a complete color surrogate of MS Ff.2.38 and exact item-level reuse terms?
next_steps: Request the whole physical object through CUL Imaging Services, not only Guy ff.161r-231r or lines 9234-36; separately verify the 1979 monochrome facsimile's availability.

open_questions.question: When will digitized content for Harley MS 3775 be restored, or can the British Library provide a mediated complete-object surrogate?
next_steps: Monitor the exact catalog and ask the British Library about f.15 and whole-object delivery; retain the relation as Zupitza's historical collation claim until the page can be inspected directly.

open_questions.question: What current U-M route exposes the 694 linked page images and an item-level rights statement without the Cloudflare challenge?
next_steps: Retry in an interactive browser or ask U-M Digital Content and Collections; do not equate historical PB references with confirmed current downloadability.
